name: OB Ultrasound First Trimester
description: First trimester obstetric ultrasound for evaluation of intrauterine pregnancy and fetal well-being

findings.technique: Transabdominal and transvaginal ultrasound was performed to evaluate the first trimester pregnancy.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Uterus
sections.content: The uterus is [anteverted / retroverted / normal position] with [normal / no fibroids / no adenomyosis] myometrial echotexture.

sections.name: Endometrium
sections.content: The endometrial complex is [appropriate for pregnancy / no abnormal findings].

sections.name: Gestational Sac
sections.content: An intrauterine gestational sac is identified measuring [X mm] in greatest dimension. The sac demonstrates [a yolk sac / fetal pole / fetal heartbeat].

sections.name: Crown-Rump Length
sections.content: The crown-rump length measures [X mm] corresponding to approximately [X weeks and X days] gestational age.

sections.name: Fetal Heart Rate
sections.content: Fetal cardiac activity is identified with a heart rate of approximately [X] beats per minute.

sections.name: Yolk Sac
sections.content: A yolk sac is identified and measures [X mm] in diameter. The yolk sac is [normal / not enlarged].

sections.name: Adnexa
sections.content: The right and left adnexa are [unremarkable / no mass / no ectopic pregnancy]. The ovaries are [visualized / normal].

sections.name: Cervix
sections.content: The cervix measures [X cm] in length and appears [closed / no cervical incompetence].

impression:
1. Intrauterine pregnancy at approximately [X] weeks by crown-rump length.
2. Fetal cardiac activity present.
3. No evidence of ectopic pregnancy or molar gestation.
author: RadReport Templates
